dialogos.texto_en: Today's main event is a visit to Shurijo Castle!
When you think of Okinawa tourism,\nyou definitely imagine this place, right?
There's more greenery than I imagined in a castle.\nWhat a nice place.
I've seen this gate before.\nThis is...Shureimon, right?
Yes, that's right.\nI think it was also depicted on a banknote.
Hey, Uesugi-san, hurry up!\nYou'll be left behiiind!
...Yeah, I'm coming now.
(I was worried about what would happen if\n the sisters went somewhere together...\n But maybe I was just being paranoid.)
(Actually, the atmosphere is even better\n after they got back.)
Uesugi-san, what's written on the sign\nhanging on the gate?
It's not a sign, it's a plaque.\nI think it says 'Shurei no Kuni.'
Shurei no Kuni?
It means, 'a country that values etiquette.'
Oooh, I see.
You obviously don't.
Hey, Fu-kun!\nLet's take a picture together\nin front of the gate!
Ah!\nThen, I'll take the picture for you.
Oh my, someone beat us to it.\nWhat should we do, Miku?
Let's follow proper etiquette and wait in line.
Huh? Just the two of us?\nWhy not take a group photo since we're all here?
Of course we will.\nBut you'll take our group photo for us, right?
Come on, Fu-kun!\nDon't talk, look at the camera!
Uesugi-san, this is a memory.\nWe're making the best memories, right?
Well...yeah.\nTaking commemorative photos\nat tourist destinations is the thing to do.
Teehee!\nThen, let's link arms.
Wait a minute!\nYou're too close!
Futaro, don't run away.
There's a line, so could we speed things up a little, okay?
Hey, don't tell me you guys as well...
Uesugi-san, smile, smile!\nOkie dokie, and here we gooo!
Hey, Fu-kun.\nLet's walk while linking arms.
If we link arms, it'll be hard to walk,\nand we'll get tired.
I-it's okay.\nIf you link arms with me,\nI can do my best, too.
Are you sure you're not already tired?
There's quite a difference in elevation\nat Shurijo Castle.\nI'm starting to get tired...
Well, we've been walking around since this morning.\nI'm also starting to feel a little tired.
If you're tired, you can come to your big sis, you know?\nI'll lend you my shoulder for free.
There's a saying that there's nothing more expensive\nthan something free, you know?
It's a tourist attraction,\nso we can just take it easy and have ourselves a stroll.\nRight, Fu-kun?
If that's the case, please let me take it easy...
You're all so SLOOOW!\nLook, we can see another gate!
Then let's go a little further to the main hall.
Ah...
What's wrong? Why did you suddenly stop?\nDid you twist your ankle or something?
No, I'm fine.
The stone walls and stone stairs...\nThat gate looks just like an actual castle.
Well, Shurijo Castle is a castle after all.\nAlthough the construction is different from the \nmainland castles, it does have a similar atmosphere.
The Yaguramon gate has a cool mountain castle vibe to it\nthat takes advantage of the surrounding terrain.
Alright, how about taking a commemorative photo\nwith the gate in the background at the staircase?
Just the two of us?
A group photo.\nIf you say you're gonna take turns again, \nit'll take too long.
Too bad.
All right, gather around, everyone.
Wow, so this is the Seiden main hall!\nIt's really luxurious!
The building's color looks beautiful \nagainst the blue sky...!
And when someone says Shurijo Castle, \nyou definitely think of this striped courtyard!
Unaaa, Uesugi-san! Unaaa!
Huh? What now?
She's talking about this courtyard.\nIt's called 'una' in the Okinawan dialect.
Oh, I see. I actually did know that,\nsince I did some research beforehand.
It's the way you were saying it before, Yotsuba.
Exactly.\nI thought you were groaning or something.\nYour interjection didn't exactly help.
Heeheehee!\nJust take it as it is, please.
Don't make me do the impossible...
Anyway, where should we go next?\nThere seems to be a lot to see.
I want to go to the Okushoin.\nIt's supposed to look like a traditional Japanese-style \nbuilding, and I want to see the garden too.
Is that the Kitaden north hall on the left?\nI think there's a shop in there, right?
Traditional Okinawan crafts are being displayed \nin the Nanden south hall on the right.\nI'd like to visit it.
...I knew it.\nWe're all going in different directions again.
It's a common problem among quintuplets.\nUesugi-san, is there anywhere in particular \nthat you want to see?
Well, you can't skip the Seiden main hall,\nbut I haven't really thought about where else to go.\nI'll just look around randomly.
Hey, Yotsuba.
Hm? What is it, Ichika?
Never mind, come over here.
What's up?
Well, it's not really my place to say.
Yotsuba, you should do what you really want to do.
Ichika...
I mean, thanks to you and the others pushing me forward,\nI finally made up my mind.
You said the same thing before.
Yes.\nBut I just wanted to say it again.\nYotsuba, you're%p-2;――
What's important now is the present,\nnot the past, right?
Huh?
Since then, a lot has happened to me too.
Ichika, I've decided not to rely on the past anymore.
That means...
The most important thing%p-2;――
Is to think about how I feel\nand what I want in the present moment.
So, it's okay.
Oh, I see.
Yes.\nThank you for worrying about me.
I'm also supporting you, Ichika.
By the way, Yotsuba, when we were talking earlier,\nwere you not looking at Futaro-kun?
Huh?!\nWhat do you mean?
Your face is turning red.
I-it's hot today, right?\nMaybe I'm feeling flushed!
Oh, look, Futaro-kun is coming this way.
WHAAA?! Umm!\nThere's somewhere I want to go,\nso let's go together. Hurry up, hurry up!
Alright, I guess I have no choice.\nI'll tag along.
Even if you've already given an answer...\nis it really okay?
I just can't handle it...
Am I the only one who's this fixated on that?
Itsuki?\nWhat are you doing spacing out here?
Uesugi-kun?! H-how long have you been there?!
What?\nIs it bad for me to call out to you?
It's not that...\nDid you...hear what I said?
Huh? About what?\nI just got here.
...You're alone.
Yeah, everyone else was looking around on their own\nand then they all disappeared.
I see.
So, did something happen?\nYou've been sighing a lot lately.
No, it's nothing.
I know what it is. You're hungry, aren't you?\nYou always get weak when you're hungry.
...I understand now how you view me, Uesugi-kun.
Since you'll be a university student soon,\nwhy don't you learn some subtlety?
S-sure.\nI'll think about it.
Anyway, for now%p-2;――
Ah! Itsuki, you're here! And I found Uesugi-san too!
My bad, I guess.
Why are you apologizing?
Well, I've been causing you trouble.
*sigh*\nSo you do care a little bit.
However, it's not simply your fault.
Anyway, for now%p-2;――
Ah! Itsuki, you're here! And I found Uesugi-san too!
Doesn't seem like it's nothing.
If there's anything you want to say to me\nor anything I can do, don't hesitate to tell me.
For someone like you,\nwho usually lacks all sense of subtlety...
It's strange how you're considerate at times like this.
Anyway, for now%p-2;――
Ah! Itsuki, you're here! And I found Uesugi-san too!
You were with Futaro-kun, huh?
Oh?\nWas I interrupting something?
Of course not.\nNot at all.
Mmm, I see.
I see. So why did you all show up here?
Well, we're almost done looking around,\nso I thought we should take a break soon.
There's a souvenir shop and a cafe nearby.\nShall we go there?
I'm worried about Itsuki's stomach too.
H-hey!\nI can still hold on!
But, they do say\nthat holding back is harmful to the body. Shall we go?
(What was Itsuki about to say?)
